rulings.Case Title: SANTOS REYES VS. CANNON CONSTRUCTORS NORTH, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 8, CLAIMANT AMERICAN CLAIMS MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion For Leave To Intervene. Off calendar. The moving party did not serve and file its moving papers at least 16 court days before the date of the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. BRIAN REED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANT BRIAN REED's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's unopposed motion to compel production of documents is granted as follows: Within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall (1) produce documents in compliance with Plaintiff's statements of compliance in Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, dated March 25, 2020 (Exhibit C to the Whang Declaration in support of the motion) and (2) pay discovery sanctions in the amount of $1,575 (4.5 hours x $350/hour). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN RAMIREZ VS. J E H ENTERPRISES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Paga Settlement; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN RAMIREZ'S Motion To Approve PAGA Settlement. Plaintiff's motion to approve PAGA settlement is granted on the condition that the amount of the gross settlement payment allocated to plaintiff's counsel's attorneys' fees is reduced to $75,000, and the $12,500 allocated to PAGA payments is increased to $25,000 (of which the LWDA shall receive 75% or $18,750 and aggrieved employees shall receive 25% or $6,250). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JANELLE WENGER VS. POLK STREET RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 3, DEFENDANT POLK STREET RESTAURANT GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, And Request For Reasonable Sanctions And Attorneys Fees Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: KEITH KENNARD VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 9, PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal. Plaintiff Kennard's motion to vacate the order sustaining demurrer entered on February 26, 2020 is denied. First, plaintiff does not sufficiently explain how the order was due to mistake, surprise, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 473. Second, plaintiff fails to demonstrate that he filed this motion "within a reasonable time" after the court's prior ruling. Plaintiff appeared at the February hearing and was aware of the court's ruling. He inexplicably waited approximately 5 months to file the instant motion even though he submitted other filings with the court in the interim (e.g. Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Arbitration Award (filed March 27, 2020).) Finally, vacating the court's prior ruling would be an idle act. Plaintiff's hybrid action seeks to vacate the parties' arbitration award and re-litigate the merits of the underlying dispute. On August 5, 2020, the court denied plaintiff's motion to vacate the arbitration award because it was untimely. Defendant is therefore entitled to confirmation and a judgment on that award. (See Berg v. Traylor (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 809, 823 ["failure to file a timely petition to vacate the arbitration award bars [the party] from challenging the judgment confirming the award on appeal. [Citations.]"].) It is now too late for plaintiff to attempt to re-litigate the underlying merits of the parties' dispute. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINA MARTINEZ ET AL VS. THE ESTERLE LLC DBA TRAVELODGE BY THE BAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Esterle Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2020, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE ESTERLE LLC, DBA TRAVELODGE BY THE BAY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Off calendar for failure to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 439, which requires the parties to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Defendant failed to file a declaration with its moving papers as required by section 439(a)(3). Counsel's August 19, 2020 declaration on reply references an attempt to meet and confer in connection with the initial case management conference, not the MJOP. =(302/EPS)
